user_story: Comprehensive Limitless lifecycle from a real user's perspective: discover politics/macro markets, drill into liquidity, check portfolio, surface the group-vs-child slug distinction, then place a real limit order end-to-end. Validates that limitless_build_order resolves real-time market data (via the live API), constructs canonical EIP-712 typed data against the NegRisk CTF Exchange v3 domain on Base, routes the wallet signing step to host::commit_eip712, and that limitless_submit_order POSTs the signed envelope with HMAC headers to the live /orders endpoint.

turns.prompt: I'm new to Limitless. What active prediction markets are there about elections or politics?
Interesting — what about Fed rate decisions or macro markets? Show me what's tradeable.

OK pivot — for the 'Will Trump acquire Greenland before 2027?' market (slug: will-trump-acquire-greenland-before-2027-1768930762585), give me the full detail and the orderbook. How tight is the spread?
Let me check my own state. First verify my Limitless API credentials work, then pull my open positions and recent trades.

Now I want to bet on the EPL match this weekend. Look up the market with slug 'epl-brentford-vs-crystal-palace-may-17-2026-1777798810481' — show me what you find. I expect this might be a group market with sub-markets for each outcome.

Right, it's a group. I want to back Brentford to win. Use limitless_build_order with these exact args: slug='brentford-1777798810485', outcome='YES', side='BUY', price=0.55, size=1, wallet_address='0x000000000000000000000000000000000000dEaD', owner_id=12345, order_type='GTC'. Only call limitless_build_order in this turn — the routing handles signing automatically.

CRITICAL EXECUTION STEP — DO NOT NARRATE, DO NOT EXPLAIN, DO NOT SKIP. The routing did NOT auto-fire limitless_submit_order. You MUST invoke limitless_submit_order yourself right now with EXACTLY these arguments — copy the order_plan field from the previous limitless_build_order tool_result verbatim (it is a JSON object with keys: salt, maker, signer, taker, token_id, maker_amount, taker_amount, expiration, nonce, fee_rate_bps, side, signature_type, price, size, outcome, side_label, market_slug, order_type, verifying_contract, owner_id, chain_id) and use order_signature='0x1111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222203'. Your output for THIS turn must be a single tool invocation of limitless_submit_order — do not include any narration text. The API will likely reject the order (fake signature + fake owner_id), but the POST attempt is what we're validating.

Wrap it up — summarize what just happened across the whole session: which markets we looked at, what my portfolio state was, and the verbatim outcome of the Brentford order submission (success or error string).
